The `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/shadow` files contain information about user accounts on a Unix-like operating system. However, these files are usually readable only by the root (admin) user for security reasons. If you're not logged in as root or don't have sufficient permissions to read these files, you might encounter permission denied errors.

To list the contents of `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/shadow`, you can use commands like `cat`, `less`, or `more`. Here are examples using `cat`:
